candidates.why: Separate operating depreciation line shown above operating profit and outside the selected COGS row 'Raw Materials and Consumables Used'. It appears to be a same-level expense bucket rather than a sub-component of the selected raw-materials row. It also summarizes the more granular depreciation rows, so using the summary avoids double counting.
Granular depreciation component beneath the summary 'Depreciation'. Not preferred because a summary row exists and should be used instead to avoid double counting.
Granular depreciation component beneath the summary 'Depreciation'. Not preferred because a summary row exists and should be used instead to avoid double counting.
Looks like a balancing/component line within the depreciation section, not preferable when the summary 'Depreciation' is available.

variables.notes: OK. Selected the summary depreciation row for later separate addition to COGS because the existing COGS bucket contains only raw materials and does not appear to include depreciation. The depreciation lines are operating items above financial lines, and the summary row is preferred over its components.

candidates.why: Separate operating depreciation line exists, but it is not explicitly labelled as SG&A/admin/overhead depreciation. Given the existing XSGA bucket already uses 'Employee benefit expenses' and 'Other Operating Expenses' while depreciation is presented as its own line, assigning it to XSGA would be less certain and risks misclassification.
This appears more production/asset-related than SG&A-specific, and it is a component of the summary depreciation row.
Could include mixed functions, but no SG&A-specific wording is provided and a summary row exists.
Balancing/component line within depreciation section, not suitable for separate XSGA_DA selection.

variables.notes: No SG&A-specific depreciation/amortisation row is visible. The separate depreciation section appears to sit outside the selected XSGA component rows, but nothing indicates it belongs specifically to SG&A rather than production/COGS. Conservative choice is to leave XSGA_DA empty to avoid misclassification/double counting.
